Nice work. You started by making yourself a real person. It's far easier to outright reject someone if they are only a name on the bottom of a contract offer. This was an anchor, though not that extreme. You're asking for $6,000 in closing costs. You do know they haven't been able to sell the home in three months, so you're likely to get their engagement in the negotiation process. You also made sure the last line on your communication was positive. The last impression is the lasting impression. Well done.
